narrative: JSW Steel delivered the strongest overall FY2024 peer-table performance after Jindal, with 34 rank-one and 32 rank-two indicators and only 9 rank-four indicators. Its ESG profile was balanced and commercially relevant. Environmentally, JSW led renewable energy and renewable electricity, with 1.98 million GJ renewable energy, and was the operational water-intensity leader at 2.39 kL/tcs while also reporting zero total water discharge. It also performed well on physical waste intensity and product-responsibility disclosures, reporting 100% coverage for both environmental/social product information and recycling or safe-disposal information. However, it was not the carbon benchmark: Jindal remained superior on GHG intensity, Scope 1, Scope 2 and air pollutants, while JSW ranked poorly on NOx. Socially, JSW was one of the two training leaders, with 100% coverage across employee and worker H&S and skills. It led worker LTIFR, reported zero employee fatalities and maintained mid-pack diversity. Its main social weakness was very low wellbeing expenditure. Governance was more mixed: JSW tied Tata on 100% R&D alignment but showed high related-party concentration in investments, loans/advances and sales. Strategically, JSW’s FY2024 position reflects a high-scale private-sector steelmaker with strong renewable procurement, water efficiency, product disclosure and training systems, but with continuing exposure to integrated steelmaking emissions, related-party flows and selected air-emission weaknesses.
